title: Siphocampylus affinis
taxonomy: Siphocampylus affinis is a species of flowering plant in the family Campanulaceae. It is a member of the Campanulaceae family, commonly known as the bellflower family. This family is a part of the larger group known as the asterids, which is a clade of flowering plants. The Campanulaceae family is further divided into several subfamilies, including the Campanuloideae subfamily. The Siphocampylus affinis is classified within the subfamily Campanuloideae.

intro: Siphocampylus affinis, also known as the "tree heath," is a flowering plant species in the family Campanulaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which have been reported to aid in wound healing and the treatment of various infections. The bioactive constituents of Siphocampylus affinis include phenolic compounds, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects.
